title: passphrase prompt text misrepresents blank-passphrase semantics
description: Prompt says 'leave blank to disable payment address generation' but blank just passes '' as the passphrase — whether it disables depends on whether the wallet was initialized with a passphrase. A user who init'd with --no-passphrase and leaves this blank gets the wallet working correctly. A user who init'd with a passphrase and accidentally presses Enter gets a decrypt failure with the correct 'disabled' message. The prompt text overpromises: it implies blank always disables, but it only disables when the decrypt fails. This will generate issue reports from users who init'd with --no-passphrase and expect 'blank = disable' to work as documented.
